description.en: As a Bonus Action, you can inspire another creature within 60 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. Once within the next hour when the creature fails a D20 Test, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the d20.

description_eval_variables.value: current_level = classes['bard']; return 'd12' if current_level >= 15; return 'd10' if current_level >= 10; return 'd8' if current_level >= 5; 'd6'

description.en: As a Bonus Action, you can inspire another creature within 60 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. Once within the next hour when the creature fails a D20 Test, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the d20. You can expend a spell slot (no action required) to regain one expended use of Bardic Inspiration.

description_eval_variables.value: current_level = classes['bard']; return 'd12' if current_level >= 15; return 'd10' if current_level >= 10; return 'd8' if current_level >= 5; 'd6'

eval_variables.skills: bonus = proficiency_bonus / 2; skills.map { |skill| next skill if skill[:level].positive?; skill[:modifier] = modifiers[skill[:ability]] + bonus; skill }

description.en: Choose {{value}} learned skills. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for selected skills.

If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can take a Reaction to cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has Advantage.

Whenever you reach a Bard level and the Prepared Spells number increases, you can choose any of your new prepared spells from the Bard, Cleric, Druid, and Wizard spell lists, and the chosen spells count as Bard spells for you. In addition, whenever you replace a spell prepared for this class, you can replace it with a spell from those lists.
When you roll Initiative, you regain expended uses of Bardic Inspiration until you have two if you have fewer than that.
You always have the Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.
You always have the Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.
You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

eval_variables.skills: selected_skill = ['arcana', 'nature']; skills.map { |skill| next skill if skill[:slug] != selected_skill; skill[:modifier] += [1, modifiers['wis']].max; skill }

description.en: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor.

As a Magic action, you can expend a spell slot or a use of Wild Shape to cast the Find Familiar spell without Material components. When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar is Fey and disappears when you finish a Long Rest.

Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required). In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a level 1 spell slot, but you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
The might of the elements flows through you.
Add +{{value}} to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.

Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).
Add +{{value}} to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip. When you cast a Druid cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet.

Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra 2d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).
While using Wild Shape, you can cast spells in Beast form, except for any spell that has a Material component with a cost specified or that consumes its Material component.
Whenever you roll Initiative and have no uses of Wild Shape left, you regain one expended use of it.

You can convert uses of Wild Shape into a spell slot (no action required). Choose a number of your unexpended uses of Wild Shape and convert them into a single spell slot, with each use contributing 2 spell levels.
You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

eval_variables.skills: selected_skill = ['arcana', 'religion']; skills.map { |skill| next skill if skill[:slug] != selected_skill; skill[:modifier] += [1, modifiers['wis']].max; skill }

description.en: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor.
When you use your Channel Divinity, you choose which effect to create.

description_eval_variables.limit: class_level = classes['cleric']; return 4 if class_level >= 18; return 3 if class_level >= 6; 2

description.en: Each Undead of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC {{value}}). If the creature fails its saving throw, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can.

Each Undead of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC {{value}}). If the creature fails its saving throw, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can. You can roll {{dice}}d8. Each Undead that fails its saving throw against that use of Turn Undead takes Radiant damage equal to the roll's total. This damage doesn't end the turn effect.

As a Magic action, you point your Holy Symbol at another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Roll {{dice}}d8+{{modifier}}. You either restore Hit Points to the creature or force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC {{value}}). On a failed save, the creature takes Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice). On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage (round down).

description_eval_variables.dice: class_level = classes['cleric']; return 4 if class_level >= 18; return 3 if class_level >= 13; return 2 if class_level >= 7; 1

description.en: Divine power infuses you in battle.
Add +{{value}} to the damage you deal with any Cleric cantrip.
Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can cause the target to take an extra 1d8 Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice).
Add +{{value}} to the damage you deal with any Cleric cantrip. When you cast a Cleric cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet.
Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can cause the target to take an extra 2d8 Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice).

As a Magic action, choose any Cleric spell of level 5 or lower that doesn't require a Reaction to cast. As part of the same action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components.

As a Magic action, choose any Cleric spell of level 5 or lower that doesn't require a Reaction to cast. As part of the same action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components. When you use your Divine Intervention feature, you can choose Wish when you select a spell. If you do so,you can't use Divine Intervention again until you finish 2d4 Long Rests.
Choose {{value}} learned skills. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for selected skills.
You know Thieves' cant and one other language of your choice.

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose.

Once per turn, you can deal an extra {{value}}d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or a Ranged weapon. You don't need Advantage on the attack roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target, the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition, and you don't have Disadvantage on the attack roll.
On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a Bonus Action: Dash, Disengage, or Hide.

As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is O until the end of the current turn.

1d6. You add a toxin to your strike, forcing the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC {{dc}}). On a failed save, the target has the Poisoned condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the Poisoned target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. To use this effect, you must have a Poisoner's Kit on your person.
1d6. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC {{dc}}) or have the Prone condition.
1d6. Immediately after the attack, you move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
2d6. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC {{dc}}), or on its next turn, it can do only one of the following: move or take an action or a Bonus Action.

6d6. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC {{dc}}), or it has the Unconscious condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. The Unconscious target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
3d6. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC {{dc}}), or it has the Blinded condition until the end of its next turn.
When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you (round down).

When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail.
Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of your skill or tool proficiencies, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage.
No attack roll can have Advantage against you unless you have the Incapacitated condition.
If you fail a D20 Test, you can turn the roll into a 20.

As a Bonus Action, you can touch a creature (which could be yourself) and draw power from the pool of healing to restore a number of Hit Points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in the pool. You can also expend 5 Hit Points from the pool of healing power to remove the Poisoned condition from the creature.
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of 2 kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice.
You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice or you learn two Cleric cantrips of your choice. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Cleric cantrip.
You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes, using it to fuel magical effects.

As a Bonus Action, you can detect Celestials, Fiends, and Undead. For the next 10 minutes you know the location of any creature of those types within 60 feet of yourself, and you know its creature type. Within the same radius, you also detect the presence ofany place or object that has been consecrated or desecrated, as with the Hallow spell.
You radiate a protective, unseeable aura in a 10-foot Emanation that originates from you. You and your allies in the aura gain a bonus to saving throws equal +{{value}}.

As a Magic action, you can expend one use of this class's Channel Divinity. You can target a number of creatures ({{value}}) that you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC {{value}}) or have the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. While Frightened in this way, a target can do only one of the following on its turns: move, take an action, or take a Bonus Action.

You radiate a protective, unseeable aura in a 10-foot Emanation that originates from you. You and your allies in the aura gain a bonus to saving throws equal +{{value}}, and have Immunity to the Frightened condition
When you hit a target with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, the target takes an extra ld8 Radiant damage.

As a Bonus Action, you can touch a creature (which could be yourself) and draw power from the pool of healing to restore a number of Hit Points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in the pool. You can also expend 5 Hit Points from the pool of healing power to remove the Poisoned, Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned condition from the creature.

You radiate a protective, unseeable aura in a 30-foot Emanation that originates from you. You and your allies in the aura gain a bonus to saving throws equal +{{value}}, and have Immunity to the Frightened condition

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of 2 kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds ofweapons you chose.
You gain Expertise in 1 skill. You know two languages of your choice.
You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice or you learn two Druid cantrips of your choice. Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Druid cantrip.
You gain Expertise in 2 skill.
As a Magic action, you can give yourself {{value}} Temporary Hit Points.
Whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level decreases by 1.
Taking damage can't break your Concentration on Hunter's Mark.
As a Bonus Action, you can give yourself the Invisible condition until the end of your next turn.
You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature cur rently marked by your Hunter's Mark.
Your connection to the forces of nature grants you Blindsight with a range of 30 feet.
The damage die of your Hunter's Mark is a d1O rather than a d6.
You can choose invocations (amount - {{value}}).

description_eval_variables.value: class_level = classes['warlock']; return ((class_level / 3) + 4) if class_level >= 9; return (((class_level + 1) / 2) + 2) if class_level >= 5; return 3 if class_level >= 2; 1

description.en: Choose 3 cantrips, and choose 2 level 1 spells that have the Ritual tag. The spells can be from any class's spell list, chosen spells are prepared.

As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand. Whenever you attack with the bonded weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity; and you can cause the weapon to deal Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage or its normal damage type.
You have Advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain Concentration.
You can see normally in Dim Light and Darkness, both magical and nonmagical, within 120 feet of yourself.
Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage. You can add +{{value}} to that spell's damage rolls.
Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage and has a range of 10+ feet. When you cast that spell, its range increases by {{value}}.
Choose one ofyour known Warlock cantrips that requires an attack roll. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with that cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from you.
Choose one ofyour known Warlock cantrips that requires an attack roll. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with that cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from you.
Choose one Origin feat.
You can use a Bonus Action to touch a willing creature and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn.
You can breathe underwater, and you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

1) The familiar gains either a Fly Speed or a Swim Speed of 40 feet. 2) As a Bonus Action, you can command the familiar to take the Attack action. 3) Whenever the familiar deals Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can make it deal Necrotic or Radiant damage instead. 4) If the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC. 5) When the familiar takes damage, you can take a Reaction to grant it Resistance against that damage.

Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can expend a Pact Magic spell slot to deal an extra 1d8 Force damage to the target, plus another 1d8 per level of the spell slot, and you can give the target the Prone condition if it is Huge or smaller.

As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand. Whenever you attack with the bonded weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity; and you can cause the weapon to deal Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage or its normal damage type. You can attack twice with the weapon instead of once when you take the Attack action on your turn.

As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand. Whenever you attack with the bonded weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity; and you can cause the weapon to deal Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage or its normal damage type. You can make 3 attacks with the weapon instead of 1 when you take the Attack action on your turn.

With your permission, a creature can take an action to write its name on that page, which can contain a number of names equal to {{value}}. When any creature whose name is on the page is reduced to O Hit Points but not killed outright, the creature magically drops to 1 Hit Point instead. Once this magic is triggered, no creature can benefit from it until you finish a Long Rest. As a Magic action, you can erase a name on the page by touching it.

Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage to the creature, and you can expend one of your Hit Point Dice to roll it and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll plus {{value}}.
You have Truesight with a range of 30 feet.
You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of it, you regain expended Pact Magic spell slots but no more than a number equal to half your maximum (round up).
You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of it, you regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots.
Choose one level 6 Warlock spell. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot.
Choose one level 6, one level 7 Warlock spell. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot.
Choose one level 6, one level 7, one level 8 Warlock spell. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot.
Choose one level 6, one level 7, one level 8, one level 9 Warlock spell. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot.
